source_description: Synthetic — deliberately broken fixture. Valid config.toml but: (1) cron/jobs.json is truncated mid-array (simulates SIGKILL during cron write), (2) one session file is truncated mid-JSON (simulates disk-full during session save), (3) config references a non-existent MCP server path. Engine must recover-and-continue, not panic.

files_sanitized.source_type: synthetic valid config with bad MCP reference

preserved: truncated mid-array structure (deliberate — tests parse error recovery)
truncated mid-object (deliberate — tests session load error recovery)

uuid_map.00000000-0004-4000-8000-000000000004: original was UUID v4 (synthetic cron job)

scenarios_that_replay_this: T6: boot with truncated cron/jobs.json — assert engine logs a parse error but continues (no panic, no exit 1)
T6: --list-sessions with one broken session file — assert valid session listed, broken one skipped with error log
T6: boot with nonexistent MCP server path — assert engine emits mcp_error event, continues without the server
T10 (chaos): SIGKILL mid-session-write produces truncated file; next boot recovers the good session
